Patch Set 1:

I agree there is a possible problem here.  I don't think this is the best solution.
This is an rhl table, meaning that there can be multiple entries with the same key.  These entries will all be linked together.  rhltable_lookup() returns the list and rhl_for_each_entry_rcu() can extract the entries.
There will still be a need to restart the walk each time an entry is deleted (I think), but we don't need to rewalk the entire table, just the short list.

I'll look at writing a patch.

Patch Set 1:

sure, thanks. my point is that this code is not very important for overall performance - I was thinking about a simple batching, but not sure that will be any better than rescan given the use case.

Abandoned

https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/40846/.. in favor of

parents.subject: LU-14097 tests: conf-sanity/91 should check specific MDT

commit.subject: LU-14161 obdclass: restart walk in obd_export_evict_by_nid()
commit.message:
LU-14161 obdclass: restart walk in obd_export_evict_by_nid()

rhashtable' comment claims that:
Also, you may miss objects if there are removals in between
rhashtable_walk_stop and the next call to rhashtable_walk_start.

this can cause obd_export_evict_by_nid() to miss exports.

Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I9d0c081accdbb63c2210116df30c20ae21db04b8
